claim_bullets.bullet_text: The way we've written .iterator as a method, each object knows how to return an iterator for itself. (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.206-209)
We could save a step and write collectionSum , a function that folds over any object, provided that the object implements an .iterator method: (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.206-209)
Note that it uses the function keyword, so when we invoke it with stack.iterator() , JavaScript sets this to the value of stack . (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.206-209)

source_record_text: This chapter explores the concept of functional iterators and how to separate the mechanism of traversing data structures from the operations performed on them.
